skill.name: goal-tracker
skill.description: Track LeanOS goal progress by deriving status, metrics, gaps, and recommendations from goal files, threads, and artifacts.
skill.summary: Derive goal status, trajectory, and next actions from LeanOS execution data.
skill.author: BellaBe

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported external commands, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns, but review found markdown examples and ordinary prose only. No executable code, network calls, shell commands, cryptographic implementation, data exfiltration, or prompt injection attempt was found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: False Positive: External Command Detections in Markdown Examples
low_findings.description: The reported external command locations are fenced markdown diagrams, pseudocode, workflow steps, and output templates. They do not invoke a shell or instruct execution of arbitrary commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are documentation code fences and workflow descriptions. No interpreter, command runner, subprocess, or shell syntax is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Detections in Prose
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings point to descriptive text and placeholder report content, not hash functions or encryption code. No cryptographic algorithm is implemented or recommended.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines contain goal-tracking descriptions and output placeholders. There is no MD5, SHA-1, cipher usage, key handling, or security-sensitive cryptographic behavior.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Reconnaissance Detections in Goal Workflow Text
low_findings.description: The system and network reconnaissance findings match section headings or data freshness instructions. The skill does not collect host, network, process, account, or environment information.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines describe hybrid autonomy behavior and stale thread data checks. No reconnaissance commands, scanning utilities, or external discovery workflow appears nearby.

content.value_statement: Goal state can drift when execution data lives across files, threads, and artifacts. This skill derives progress, gaps, trajectory, and recommended actions from the current LeanOS workspace.
actual_capabilities: Reads LeanOS goal files, linked threads, and artifacts to derive current goal state.
Calculates active, completed, and blocked thread counts for each goal.
Maps completed thread outcomes to success criteria and progress metrics.
Projects goal trajectory against deadlines and target metrics.
Identifies gaps and recommends actions based on goal autonomy settings.
Updates goal state sections and produces progress reports when requested.
limitations: Requires LeanOS goal and thread conventions to be present in the workspace.
Depends on current and accurate thread data for reliable progress calculations.
Does not change goal objectives or success criteria; that belongs to goal-setter.
Should not create threads unless the goal autonomy setting allows it.

use_cases.title: Review weekly progress
use_cases.description: Generate a current status report with progress, risks, gaps, and next recommended actions.

use_cases.title: Update goals after thread completion
use_cases.description: Apply completed thread outcomes to linked goal metrics and subgoal status.

use_cases.title: Detect execution gaps
use_cases.description: Find stalled metrics, blocked subgoals, stale data, and missed trajectory early.

prompt_templates.title: Check one goal
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner status check for a single goal.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the goal-tracker skill to review my active goal named {goal_name}. Report status, progress, gaps, and the next recommended action.

prompt_templates.title: Create a goal snapshot
prompt_templates.scenario: Review all active goals at once.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use goal-tracker to scan all active LeanOS goals and produce a concise snapshot with risk level, progress, stale data, and recommended actions.

prompt_templates.title: Update after thread completion
prompt_templates.scenario: Apply new execution evidence to a linked goal.
prompt_templates.prompt: A thread just reached Stage 6 learning. Use goal-tracker to update the linked goal state, subgoal status, metrics, and log entry.

prompt_templates.title: Analyze trajectory and interventions
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review before planning or prioritization.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use goal-tracker to calculate trajectory for {goal_id}, compare projected completion to the deadline, identify the largest gaps, and propose interventions based on autonomy mode.

output_examples.output: Status: At Risk. Progress is below the target trajectory, pipeline is flat, and one thread is blocked. Recommended action: create or approve a pipeline recovery thread.

output_examples.input: Update the goal linked to the completed sales-process thread.
output_examples.output: The linked subgoal is now in progress, completed thread count increased, conversion learning was added to metrics, and the goal log received a dated update.
Two active goals have not changed in more than seven days. Both need fresh thread data before reliable trajectory decisions can be made.

best_practices: Keep goal files, thread metadata, and Stage 6 learning current before running reviews.
Confirm autonomy settings before allowing the skill to create gap-closing threads.
Run trajectory checks before planning sessions and after meaningful execution updates.
anti_patterns: Do not use it to rewrite objectives, deadlines, or success criteria without goal-setter review.
Do not mark a goal complete unless every success criterion has verified evidence.
Do not rely on reports when linked threads are stale, missing, or contradictory.

faq.question: What does this skill track?
faq.answer: It tracks LeanOS goal progress, execution status, metrics, gaps, trajectory, and recommended actions.

faq.question: Does it create new goals?
faq.answer: No. It tracks existing goals and may recommend refinement through the goal-setter workflow.

faq.question: Can it update files?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill describes updates to goal state sections, logs, subgoal status, and completed goal movement.

faq.question: Can it create new execution threads?
faq.answer: Only when a gap exists and the goal autonomy setting allows automatic or hybrid action.

faq.question: What data does it need?
faq.answer: It needs LeanOS goal files, linked thread metadata, thread status, Stage 6 learning, and artifact evidence.

faq.question: Is this a general habit tracker?
faq.answer: No. It is designed for LeanOS operational goals that use files, threads, metrics, and success criteria.
